typography.import_instructions: Import 'Cairo' (Weights: 400, 700, 900) and 'Almarai' (Weights: 400, 700) from Google Fonts in index.css

buttons.default: rounded-lg px-6 py-3 font-bold transition-all active:scale-95

inputs.default: bg-slate-900 border-slate-700 focus:border-lime-400 rounded-lg text-white placeholder:text-slate-500

cards.room_card: relative overflow-hidden rounded-xl border border-slate-800 hover:border-lime-400/50 transition-all group cursor-pointer h-48

layout.container: max-w-md mx-auto min-h-screen bg-slate-950 relative overflow-hidden shadow-2xl
layout.navigation: fixed bottom-0 w-full max-w-md bg-slate-900/90 backdrop-blur-xl border-t border-slate-800 p-4 flex justify-around z-50

universal_guidelines: Always assume Dark Mode. Do not implement a light mode toggle unless specifically asked.
Use 'Cairo' font for all Arabic text to maintain the modern sports feel.
Ensure high contrast for accessibility: Lime-400 on Slate-900 is excellent.
Room cards should have a dark gradient overlay (bg-gradient-to-t from-slate-950 to-transparent) to ensure text readability over images.
Voice indicators are critical: Use the 'speaking' ring animation when a user is talking.
Use Lucide-React icons with stroke-width={1.5} for a sleek look.
Keep the UI 'App-like': Hide scrollbars, use fixed headers/footers where appropriate for a mobile feel.
instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Install 'framer-motion' for animations. 2. Add the Google Fonts link to public/index.html immediately. 3. Update tailwind.config.js to include the custom colors (slate-950, lime-400). 4. Create a 'Layout' component that enforces the mobile-app width limit on desktop (max-w-md mx-auto) to simulate a mobile experience.
